name: What to Do Immediately After a Forklift Accident: A 5-Step Guide to Protecting Your Rights
description: What to Do Immediately After a Forklift Accident: A 5-Step Guide to Protecting Your Rights - Boise Idaho

step.name: Step 1
step.text: A forklift accident is a chaotic and frightening event. Whether you were pinned, struck, or involved in a tip-over, the steps you take in the first hour and days can determine whether your medical bills are paid or if you're left with thousands of dollars in debt. Use this guide to navigate the immediate aftermath of a forklift injury.

step.name: Step 3
step.text: Your health is the first priority. Even if you don't feel 'broken,' forklift accidents involve massive amounts of weight and force that can cause internal bleeding, concussions, or spinal compression that isn't immediately obvious. Go to the nearest emergency room or industrial clinic. **Critical:** Tell the doctor exactly how it happened and ensure they record that it was a forklift accident on the job.

step.name: Step 4
step.text: ### 2. Report the Accident to Your Supervisor

step.name: Step 5
step.text: In states like Utah, you have a 30-day limit to report the injury, but waiting is a mistake. Report it as soon as you are physically able. Do this in writing—a text or email is better than a phone call. Use clear language: "I was injured today while operating the forklift." This creates an indisputable timestamp.

step.name: Step 7
step.text: While the scene is still fresh, take photos of the forklift, its controls, any visible damage, and the surroundings (spilled liquids, blocked aisles, etc.). If there were coworkers nearby, get their names. Construction and warehouse sites change quickly; the evidence of a mechanical failure or an unsafe floor could be gone by the next shift.

step.name: Step 9
step.text: Try to identify the make, model, and serial number of the forklift. If the brakes failed or the mast malfunctioned, this information is vital. It allows your legal team to investigate whether the equipment was recalled or if a third-party maintenance company was negligent in their repairs.

step.name: Step 10
step.text: ### 5. Consult a Workers' Comp Attorney Before Signing Anything

step.name: Step 11
step.text: The insurance adjuster for your employer may contact you within 24 hours. They may sound helpful, but their goal is to close the file cheaply. Do not sign 'authorization' forms or give recorded statements until you have spoken with a lawyer. An attorney ensures that the full extent of your injury is recognized, including future medical needs.

step.name: Step 12
step.text: ### Conclusion: Don't Wait for the Pain to Worsen

step.name: Step 13
step.text: The most common mistake injured workers make is trying to 'tough it out.' This only creates gaps in medical treatment that the insurance company will use against you. By following these five steps, you build a fortress of evidence around your claim, ensuring you get the medical care and lost wages you deserve.
